title: Phalacrodiscus pallens
intro: Phalacrodiscus pallens, also known as False Dill, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and parts of Central America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Phalacrodiscus pallens include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with False Dill include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and provide antioxidant protection.
taxonomy: Phalacrodiscus pallens is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is further classified within the subfamily Barnadesioideae and tribe Barnadesieae. The genus Phalacrodiscus is part of the tribe Barnadesieae, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The classification of Phalacrodiscus pallens is primarily based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. Further research may be required to fully understand the classification and relationships of this plant.
